title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Term Insurance for NRI
A term insurance plan provides financial security against the risk of premature demise. It is an important risk management tool that can help you take care of your family members in your absence. The payout received from the term plan can not only provide your family the financial means to meet their daily needs, but it can also help them in fulfilling their financial goals.
Just like resident Indians, Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) might also need a term insurance policy for financial planning
What is Term Insurance for NRI Plans?
An NRI term insurance plan is a life insurance policy specifically designed for Indian citizens residing outside India. It works just like any other term plan, where you get a sum assured, set a policy term, and the mode of payment for the premiums.
You can buy a term plan for NRI customers online from your place of residence or during a visit to India. With NRI term insurance, your family in India stays financially protected.
Reasons to Buy Term insurance for NRIs
Here are some reasons why NRIs can consider buying a term insurance plan in India [2] and[3]–
01 Financial security
Firstly, term insurance plans provide financial security against the risk of premature demise. Thus, NRIs looking to provide for their families back home in their absence can buy these plans. The term plan would ensure that in the absence of the life assured, the financial payout would help the family face the financial loss suffered.
02 Low premiums
Term plans in India are comparatively lower priced compared to term plans available internationally. Thus, NRIs can save on the premium outgo while enjoying financial protection against the risk of premature demise.
03 Wider scope of coverage
Term plans in India offer a high sum assured level for optimal coverage. Moreover, modern-day term plans offer built-in rider benefits that enhance the scope of coverage.
04 Tax benefits
Lastly, the tax benefit angle helps NRIs save tax on income earned in India. By buying a term insurance plan, NRIs can apply for an Income Tax deduction for the premium paid. The deduction allowed is up to ₹1.5 lakhs u/s 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961, subject to specified conditions under old tax regime. Moreover, the death benefit received from the plan is also tax-exempt.
If NRIs choose a return of premium plan, the maturity benefit would also enjoy tax exemption under Section 10(10D) provided specified terms and conditions are fulfilled.
Features of Term Insurance for NRIs
Some of the salient features of term plans  are as follows –
NRIs can choose an optimal coverage based on their needs.
A long-term policy tenure is allowed for maximum protection. Some term plans might also offer the whole life feature wherein the coverage runs up to 99 or 100 years of age.
You can choose to pay the premium regularly, for a limited tenure or in one lump sum.
The available premium payment frequency includes annual, monthly, quarterly, or half-yearly.
Optional riders are available at an additional premium and can be added to the policy either at the time of purchase or on subsequent renewals.
Some term plans might also allow you to increase the sum assured on specific events like marriage and childbirth. This helps you enhance the coverage to meet the increased financial responsibilities[6].
Types of NRI Term Insurance Plans
Term insurance plans available in India can be taken by both resident Indians and NRIs.
When it comes to term insurance plans for NRIs, there are four types of plans available. These are as follows[1]–
Level term plans – these are the basic term plans where the sum assured remains constant throughout the policy term.
Increasing term plans – under these plans, the sum assured increases during the policy term either by a specified percentage or by a specific amount.
Decreasing term plans – under these plans, the sum assured reduces during the policy term. These plans are usually issued with loans wherein the decreasing sum assured corresponds to the decreasing value of the outstanding loan amount.
Term plans with return of premium – normal term insurance plans usually do not pay any benefit if the term expires and the life assured is alive. However, under these plans, the premiums paid during the policy term are refunded back on maturity.
Why Buying a Term Insurance Plan from India Makes Sense for NRIs?
Opting for a term life insurance for NRI customers from India comes with many advantages like lower premiums, better flexibility, and familiarity with Indian banking.
Wide Range of Options
You can compare plans using a term insurance calculator and pick one that best matches your age, income, and family needs.
Worldwide Coverage with 24x7 Claim Assistance
No matter where you live, the coverage you get is global. The claim process is initiated in India, and support is available 24X7 via dedicated online support sections or through local partner branches.
Tele/Video Medical Check-Ups
Several insurers in India offer telemedical or video-based check-ups for NRIs. This eliminates the need for in-person exams, especially if you're abroad and unable to visit a lab. This level of convenience is a major benefit of choosing NRI term plans.
High Claim Settlement Ratio
Indian insurers have a strong reputation for claims processing. This high ratio is a big reason why most NRIs choose plans within India. It helps to build a level of trust and assurance for their families.
Easy Claim Process
Claim filing can be done online on the official website, a mobile app, or at an Indian branch. Your nominee can scan the documents and upload them online, making the process stress-free for them.
Low Premium Rates
Premiums for term insurance in India are typically more cost-optimized as compared to popular international plans.
GST Refund Benefit
If you pay the premium through an NRE account or in foreign currency, you may be eligible for a GST waiver/refund, subject to documentation and policy rules.
How does term insurance for NRIs work?
Under a term plan for NRIs, you choose the following policy details –
The sum assured
Policy term
Premium paying term
Premium paying frequency
Optional riders, if any
Based on these details, your age and other risk factors, the premium is determined. You pay the premium over the chosen premium paying term and in the selected frequency.
During the policy tenure, if the life assured passes away, the death benefit is paid. On the other hand, if the policy matures and the life assured is alive, no maturity benefit is paid. However, if you have opted for the return of premium term plan, your premiums would be refunded on maturity.
Documents required for term insurance plans for NRIs
Most of the documents required for buying a term insurance plan are similar for resident Indians and NRIs. However, some additional documents might be needed for NRIs. The common documents include the following –
Completely filled Proposal form
Passport copy
Age proof, Valid KYC documents, PAN/Form 60
NRI & Foreign travel questionnaire
Tax Identification number (TIN) of residing country or equivalent details
NRE bank statements & standard income documents subject to requirement
Supporting documents incase of GST waiver benefit required
Eligibility criteria for buying term insurance plan for NRIs in India
NRIs have to qualify  the eligibility parameters specified by the insurance company to buy a term insurance plan in India. Some of the common eligibility parameters for buying a term insurance plan are as follows –
Age – There is a minimum and maximum age bracket for buying term plans. NRIs falling within this bracket can buy the policy.
Income – NRIs should have a steady source of income to pay the premium of the policy
How do you purchase term insurance for NRIs in India?
NRIs can buy a term insurance policy in India either online or offline. NRIs can even buy the policy from abroad. In that case, they will have to send the relevant documents through mail to the insurance company, they can undergo medical tests at the available medical facility outside India and share the reports over mail, or undergo telemedical as required by the insurer. Alternatively, NRIs can buy a term insurance plan when they are visiting India.
To pay the premium, you can make the payment in cash, cheque or demand draft when you are in India. Alternatively, you can also make online payments to buy the plan. The premium has to be paid in Indian Rupee (INR). As such, you should have an NRO or NRE Bank Account in India for premium payments.
FAQs on Term Insurance for NRIs in India
1. Is an NRI eligible to buy term insurance
Yes, NRIs can buy term insurance in India for their coverage needs provided they fulfil the eligibility parameters of the plan that they choose to buy.
2. What are the factors to be considered while buying term plans for NRIs in India?
While buying a term plan for NRIs in India, here are some factors that can be considered –
The sum assured of the policy – It should be optimal to provide sufficient financial assistance to your family.
The premium – it should be affordable and competitive
Optional riders – you can choose optional riders to enhance the scope of coverage of your policy
Policy term – choose a term for which you want to be insured. Ideally, long-term coverage is recommended for maximum protection.
3. Is it necessary for NRI to be present in India to purchase term insurance?
NRIs can buy a term insurance plan from abroad too. It is not necessary for them to be present in India. If they are buying from abroad, they have to send the required documents for the purchase by mail and pay the premium online.
4. Why should NRIs invest in insurance in India?
NRIs can opt for an insurance policy in India to provide financial protection to their loved ones as well as avail tax benefits prescribed under the Income Tax Act, 1961. Moreover, insurance plans in India might offer lower premiums compared to international plans which prove cost-effective for NRIs.
5. How can NRI claim term insurance in India?
NRIs can follow the claim process and claim their term insurance benefits in India. They have to notify the insurance company of their claim, submit the necessary documents and the claim will be settled
6. Are there any tax benefits in NRI term plans?
NRIs can enjoy tax deductions under Section 80C on term insurance premiums paid under old tax regime. However, the premium must be less than 10% of the sum assured[5]
7. Can NRIs purchase term insurance without medical examination?
In order to purchase a term insurance plan, the NRI must undergo a medical examination or telemedical as required by the insurer. Upon reviewing the final reports, the insurer will take the final call on policy issuance.[4] Thus, buying a term plan without medical examination might be difficult.
8. Can NRIs transfer the term insurance policy to another person?
A policy, once bought, cannot be transferred in the name of the other person. The NRI will be the life insured over the policy tenure. However, NRIs can assign the policy in another’s name, in which case the title of the policy will change. In the case of assignment, the policy benefits will be paid to the assignee (the individual in whose name the policy has been assigned).
9. Can PIOs (Persons of Indian Origin) purchase term insurance in India?
Yes, PIOs can buy term insurance plans in India, provided they fulfil the eligibility parameters specified under the policy.
10. Can OCIs (Overseas Citizens of India) purchase term insurance in India?
Yes, OCIs can buy a term insurance policy in India if they fulfil the eligibility parameters specified under the policy they choose
11. What happens to your existing insurance when you become an NRI?
Your existing life insurance policy stays active even after you become an NRI. You don’t need to cancel or change it unless your insurer mentions this as a mandatory requirement. Inform the company about your new residency status and submit updated KYC documents. Keep using your NRO or NRE account to continue premium payments smoothly. Make sure your nominee details are up to date and your contact details are accessible from your current location.
12. What are NRE and NRO accounts for Non-Resident Indians?
An NRE (Non-Resident External) account holds your foreign income in India. You can use it to pay for your NRI life insurance policy and even repatriate funds tax-free. An NRO (Non-Resident Ordinary) account holds income earned in India like rent, interest, or dividends and is subject to Indian taxes.
13. Does term insurance cover outside India?
Yes, your Indian term life insurance for NRI coverage extends worldwide. If you die during the policy term while living or travelling abroad, your nominee can still raise a claim in India. The claim will be settled in Indian rupees as long as your policy was active and all conditions were fulfilled.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions, please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy

A term insurance plan provides financial security against the risk of premature demise. It is an important

Just like resident Indians, Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) might also need a term insurance policy for financial
planning

Thus, NRIs looking to provide for their families back home in their absence can buy these plans. offer the whole life feature wherein the coverage runs up to 99 or 100 years of age.
3. You can choose to pay the premium regularly, for a limited tenure or in one lump sum.

yearly.

marriage and childbirth. This helps you enhance the coverage to meet the increased financial

follows[1]–

either by a specified percentage or by a specific amount.
3. Decreasing term plans – under these plans, the sum assured reduces during the policy term.
These plans are usually issued with loans wherein the decreasing sum assured corresponds
to the decreasing value of the outstanding loan amount.
4. Term plans with return of premium – normal term insurance plans usually do not pay any
benefit if the term expires and the life assured is alive. However, under these plans, the
premiums paid during the policy term are refunded back on maturity.
Why Buying a Term Insurance Plan from India Makes Sense for NRIs?
Opting for a term life insurance for NRI customers from India comes with many advantages like lower premiums, better flexibility etc.
Wide Range of Options
You can compare plans using a term insurance calculator and pick one that best matches your age, income, and family needs.
Worldwide Coverage with 24x7 Claim Assistance
No matter where you live, the coverage you get is global. The claim process is initiated in India, and support is available 24X7 via dedicated online support sections or through local partner branches.
Tele/Video Medical Check-Ups
Several insurers in India offer telemedical or video-based check-ups for NRIs. This eliminates the need for in-person exams, especially if you're abroad and unable to visit a lab. This level of convenience is a major benefit of choosing NRI term plans.
High Claim Settlement Ratio
Indian insurers have a strong reputation for claims processing. This high ratio is a big reason why most NRIs choose plans within India. It helps to build a level of trust and assurance for their families.
Easy Claim Process
Claim filing can be done online on the official website, a mobile app, or at an Indian branch. Your nominee can scan the documents and upload them online, making the process stress-free for them.
Low Premium Rates
Premiums for term insurance in India are typically more cost-optimized as compared to popular international plans.
GST Refund Benefit
If you pay the premium through an NRE account or in foreign currency, you may be eligible for a GST waiver/refund, subject to documentation and policy rules.
How does term insurance for NRIs work?
Under a term plan for NRIs, you choose the following policy details –
1. The sum assured
2. Policy term
3. Premium paying term
4. Premium paying frequency
5. Optional riders, if any
Based on these details, your age and other risk factors, the premium is determined. You pay the premium
over the chosen premium paying term and in the selected frequency.
During the policy tenure, if the life assured passes away, the death benefit is paid. On the other hand, if
the policy matures and the life assured is alive, no maturity benefit is paid. However, if you have opted
for the return of premium term plan, your premiums would be refunded on maturity.
Documents required for term insurance plans for NRIs
Most of the documents required for buying a term insurance plan are similar for resident Indians and
NRIs. However, some additional documents might be needed for NRIs. The common documents include
the following –
1. Completely filled Proposal form
2. A valid passport copy
3. Age proof, Valid KYC documents, PAN/Form 60
4. NRI & Foreign travel questionnaire
5. FATCA/CRS declaration with Tax Identification number (TIN) of residing country or equivalent
details
6. NRE bank statements & standard income documents subject to requirement
7. Supporting documents in case of GST waiver benefit required
Eligibility criteria for buying term insurance plan for NRIs in India
NRIs have to qualify the eligibility parameters specified by the insurance company to buy a term insurance

–
1. Age – There is a minimum and maximum age bracket for buying term plans. NRIs falling
within this bracket can buy the policy.
2. Income – NRIs should have a steady source of income to pay the premium of the policy
How do you purchase term insurance for NRIs in India?
NRIs can buy a term insurance policy in India either online or offline. NRIs can even buy the policy from
abroad. In that case, they will have to send the relevant documents through mail to the insurance
company, they can undergo medical tests at the available medical facility outside India and share the
reports over mail, or undergo tele-medical as required by the insurer. Alternatively, NRIs can buy a term
insurance plan when they are visiting India.
To pay the premium, you can make the online payment through various digital modes from your NRE/NRO
account, cheque or demand draft when you are in India. Alternatively, you can also make online payments
to buy the plan. The premium has to be paid in Indian Rupee (INR). As such, you should have an NRO or
NRE Bank Account in India for premium payments.
FAQs on Term Insurance for NRIs in India
1. Is an NRI eligible to buy term insurance Yes, NRIs can buy term insurance in India for their coverage needs provided they fulfil the eligibility parameters of the plan that they choose to buy.
2. What are the factors to be considered while buying term plans for NRIs in India? While buying a term plan for NRIs in India, here are some factors that can be considered –
1. The sum assured of the policy – It should be optimal to provide sufficient financial assistance to
your family. 2. The premium – it should be affordable and competitive 3. Optional riders – you can choose optional riders to enhance the scope of coverage of your policy 4. Policy term – choose a term for which you want to be insured. Ideally, long-term coverage is
recommended for maximum protection.
3. Is it necessary for NRI to be present in India to purchase term insurance? NRIs can buy a term insurance plan from abroad too. It is not necessary for them to be present in India. If they are buying from abroad, they have to send the required documents for the purchase by mail and pay the premium online.
4. Why should NRIs invest in insurance in India? NRIs can opt for an insurance policy in India to provide financial protection to their loved ones as well as avail tax benefits prescribed under the Income Tax Act, 1961. Moreover, insurance plans in
India might offer lower premiums compared to international plans which prove cost-effective for NRIs.
5. How can NRI claim term insurance in India? NRIs can follow the claim process and claim their term insurance benefits in India. They have to notify the insurance company of their claim, submit the necessary documents and the claim will be settled
6. Are there any tax benefits in NRI term plans? NRIs can enjoy tax deductions under Section 80C on term insurance premiums paid under old tax regime. However, the premium must be less than 10% of the sum assured[5]
7. Can NRIs purchase term insurance without medical examination? In order to purchase a term insurance plan, the NRI must undergo a medical examination or tele- medical as required by the insurer. Upon reviewing the final reports, the insurer will take the final call on policy issuance.[4] Thus, buying a term plan without medical examination might be difficult.
8. Can NRIs transfer the term insurance policy to another person?
A policy, once bought, cannot be transferred in the name of the other person. The NRI will be the life insured over the policy tenure. However, NRIs can assign the policy in another’s name, in which case the title of the policy will change. In the case of assignment, the policy benefits will be paid to the assignee (the individual in whose name the policy has been assigned).
9. Can PIOs (Persons of Indian Origin) purchase term insurance in India? Yes, PIOs can buy term insurance plans in India, provided they fulfil the eligibility parameters specified under the policy.
10. Can OCIs (Overseas Citizens of India) purchase term insurance in India?
Yes, OCIs can buy a term insurance policy in India if they fulfil the eligibility parameters specified under the policy they choose
11. What happens to your existing insurance when you become an NRI?
Your existing life insurance policy stays active even after you become an NRI. You don’t need to cancel or change it unless your insurer mentions this as a mandatory requirement. Inform the company about your new residency status and submit updated KYC documents. Keep using your NRO or NRE account to
continue premium payments smoothly. Make sure your nominee details are up to date and your contact details are accessible from your current location.
12. What are NRE and NRO accounts for Non-Resident Indians?
An NRE (Non-Resident External) account holds your foreign income in India. You can use it to pay for your NRI life insurance policy and even repatriate funds tax-free. An NRO (Non-Resident Ordinary) account holds income earned in India like rent, interest, or dividends and is subject to Indian taxes.
13. Does term insurance cover outside India?
Yes, your Indian term life insurance for NRI coverage extends worldwide. If you die during the policy term while living or travelling abroad, your nominee can still raise a claim in India. The claim will be settled in Indian rupees as long as your policy was active and all conditions were fulfilled.
